title: hylodesmum podocarpum subsp. oxyphyllum
intro: Hylodesmum podocarpum subsp. oxyphyllum, also known as the New Zealand kawakawa tree, is a shrub or small tree native to the North Island of New Zealand. The leaves of this plant contain a high concentration of polyphenolic compounds, particularly kawakawin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Hylodesmum podocarpum subsp. oxyphyllum include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, including pain relief, digestive issues, and skin problems. Hylodesmum podocarpum subsp. oxyphyllum is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to reap its numerous health benefits.
